sections.paragraph:
pl. prop. turnings; see the root No. 1, whence causes, circumstances, Umftände, reasons, affairs; comp. سَبَبُ cause, from the root סָבַב to turn, to turn round; حَالُ way, manner, cause, from حَاَلَ, חוּל to be turned; Germ. um for wegen; בִּגְלַל on account of, from גָּלַל. It is only used in the phrase עַל־אֹודֹות i.q. עַל דְּבַר, עַל דִּבְרֵי on account of the causes, i.e. on account of, Gen 21:11, 25 Gen 21:25 26:32 Exo 18:8, and with suff. עַל אֹודֹותַי for my sake, Jos 14:6. עַל כָּל־אֹדֹות אֲשֶׁר “for these very causes that,” gerade deshalb weil, Jer 3:8. As to the reading of the editions in 2Sa 13:16, אַל אֹודֹות it appears to have arisen from the blending of two readings, the one אֶל אֹודֹות, the other עַל אֹודֹות.

I.
